w:r.w:t: AWS Textract / Azure Form Recognizer (provider-agnostic via CMT)

Feature B4 — Document Management & Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) — is the end-to-end document engine of the KT Onboarding Enterprise platform. It eliminates paper-based, email-driven, and manually rekeyed document processes by delivering template-driven generation, multi-party e-signature, OCR-powered ingestion, a secure per-onboardee document vault, and bulk-send capability — all natively within Salesforce, with no OmniStudio dependency.

B4 is a horizontal enabler for KT Onboarding. Every other feature — B5 (Identity Verification), B6 (Compliance Engine), B9 (Training & LMS), B13 (Personas) — depends on B4 to generate, store, and route documents. It is the document backbone of the entire platform.
Documents emailed as attachments; rekeyed manually into HR systems
200+ templates auto-generated with Salesforce merge fields — zero rekeying
No version control on offer letters, NDAs, or policy documents
Full document versioning with audit trail — every version stored in vault
Multi-party signing managed via email chains; no expiry enforcement
Automated signing order, delegation, expiry handling, and completion tracking
Uploaded passports and certificates parsed manually by HR
OCR auto-extracts structured data from scanned IDs and certificates
Bulk onboarding cohorts (100+ starters) took days to dispatch documents
Batch Apex processes 1,000+ simultaneous document sends asynchronously
Compliance audits required manual document assembly
Audit-ready document vault per onboardee — all docs versioned and timestamped
200+ pre-built DOCX/PDF templates across all 8 KT domains — ready out-of-the-box
Dynamic generation using Salesforce data merge — no manual field population
KT Sign (native e-signature) included from Professional tier — no DocuSign license required for basic use
OCR ingestion via AWS Textract or Azure Form Recognizer — provider switchable via Custom Metadata
Bulk send to 1,000+ onboardees simultaneously via Batch Apex with Queueable chaining
Fully integrated with B1 (DIE), B5 (Verification), B6 (Compliance), B9 (Training) for end-to-end document lifecycle
AppExchange-safe: managed package with namespace protection, upgrade-safe metadata
DIE auto-selects document templates based on domain + persona; triggers B4 document checklist creation
KT Sign (B4) captures e-signatures within Smart Forms; form submissions trigger document generation
Uploaded identity documents (passports, licenses) flow into B4 OCR pipeline for structured data extraction
Compliance task completion triggers document generation (e.g., HIPAA consent, OSHA acknowledgment)
Onboardees upload documents via portal; HR dispatches signed documents back through portal vault view
Certificate auto-generation on course pass uses B4 Document Generator; cert stored in document vault
Document completion rates, signing SLA breaches, vault completeness fed into CRM Analytics dashboards
Each persona has a dedicated document checklist defined in B4 Custom Metadata; composite personas merge checklists
Category: Documents │ Tech Layer: LWC + Apex + Salesforce Flow │ Status: ★ Newly Added
The following table documents every sub-feature and micro-capability within B4. Each is individually addressed in the technical design sections that follow.
Document Template Library — 200+ DOCX/PDF templates across 8 domains
Dynamic document generation — Salesforce data merge fields (Apex-powered)
Real-time document preview before generation (ktDocumentPreview LWC)
KT Sign — native canvas-based e-signature (touch + stylus)
E-signature delegation — signer unavailable → alternate authorized signer
E-signature expiry — auto-escalation after configurable expiry period
E-signature audit trail — IP, timestamp (UTC), device UA, geolocation
DocuSign add-on integration (envelope API, status webhooks, completed doc retrieval)
Document versioning — full version history per document
Document access control — role-based visibility within vault
Document expiry tracking — certs, licenses, and compliance docs with expiry date
OCR document ingestion — passport, ID, certificate → structured Salesforce data
OCR provider: AWS Textract (configurable via CMT)
OCR provider: Azure Form Recognizer (configurable via CMT)
OCR field mapping — configurable per document type via KT_OCR_FieldMapping__mdt
OCR review UI — HR one-click accept/reject before writing to onboarding record
Bulk document send — 1,000+ simultaneous onboardees via Batch Apex
Batch document status tracking — per-record status with failure logging
Document checklist auto-generation by DIE on onboarding record creation
Required vs. optional document classification per persona/domain
Document request reminders — Flow-triggered via email/SMS/in-portal
Agentforce HR Copilot integration — OCR discrepancy flagging
Agentforce Compliance Auditor integration — missing document gap alerts
Trusted Agent Identity for SOX/HIPAA high-value signature authorization
AXL — signing tiles rendered natively in Slack/Teams
Data Cloud streaming — document vault events to unified onboardee profile

B4 is composed of five architectural layers: Document Generation, E-Signature Orchestration, OCR Ingestion, Document Vault, and Bulk Processing. Each layer is fully Salesforce-native, built on LWC + Apex + Salesforce Flow — with no OmniStudio components.

All guided experiences previously handled by OmniScript are replaced with Salesforce Screen Flows (for step-by-step HR workflows) and LWC (for real-time interactive UI). All external API callouts previously handled by OmniStudio Integration Procedures are replaced with Apex HttpCallout classes using Named Credentials. This maintains full governor limit safety and upgrade compatibility.
Document preview, vault browsing, signing configuration, OCR review UI
Step-by-step document dispatch, signing order configuration, bulk send wizard
Compliance document triggers, expiry reminders, signing escalation, vault status updates
Document generation (DOCX/PDF), e-signature orchestration, OCR API calls, bulk processing
Asynchronous bulk document generation and dispatch to 1,000+ onboardees
DocuSign API, Adobe Sign API, AWS Textract, Azure Form Recognizer — all via Named Credentials
Async document upload events, OCR completion events, signing completion events
OCR discrepancy flagging, missing document gap alerts, natural-language document status queries
Document vault events streamed to unified onboardee profile for compliance scoring
Namespace-protected, upgrade-safe deployment to subscriber orgs
The flow below describes the end-to-end document lifecycle for a typical new employee onboarding scenario:
On KT_Onboarding__c insert: query KT_Document_Template__mdt for domain/persona, create KT_Document_Request__c records
HR opens onboarding record, previews auto-selected document templates with merged Salesforce data
HR triggers generation — Apex merges data, creates ContentVersion (DOCX/PDF), links to KT_DocumentVault__c
HR configures signing order, signer roles, expiry via guided Screen Flow
Signing invitation dispatched via Email Template + Messaging Channel; KT_Signature_Request__c created
Experience Cloud Portal (B7) or external KT Sign URL
Onboardee opens signing link, reviews document, applies canvas signature via ktSignaturePad LWC
Signature stored as ContentVersion; KT_Signature_Audit__c created; Platform Event published
KT_DocumentVault__c status updated; KT_Document_Request__c marked complete; B6 Compliance Check updated
Onboardee uploads passport/certificate; Platform Event KT_Document_Uploaded__e published
OCR API called (AWS Textract or Azure Form Recognizer via Named Credential); structured JSON returned
HR reviews extracted data, accepts/rejects field-by-field before writing to onboarding record
Copilot reviews OCR results vs. existing record data, flags discrepancies; alerts HR via in-app notification
B4 introduces 8 new custom objects, 4 Custom Metadata Types, and extends 2 existing KT objects. All objects use the KT__ namespace in the managed package.
Created by the DIE at onboarding record creation. One record per required document per onboardee. Drives the document checklist in the portal and HR UI.
Points to the template that must be generated or collected
Not Started / In Progress / Awaiting Signature / Complete / Waived / Rejected
If true, document must be complete before journey stage progression (Mandatory enforcement)
Calculated from domain/persona SLA; used for reminder triggers and overdue detection
Outbound (KT generates and sends) / Inbound (onboardee uploads) / Both
Populated by HR if document is waived with justification; immutable once set
Timestamp when document status changed to Complete — formula-populated
Link to the actual stored document once complete
Number of reminders sent — incremented by Scheduled Flow for reminder throttling
Stores the binary template file and all configuration metadata. One record per template. Managed by HR Admins or delivered via managed package.
Human-readable name — e.g., 'UK Employment Contract — Healthcare New Employee'
Reference to the DOCX or PDF template file stored in Salesforce Files
DOCX / PDF / Both — determines generated file format
Comma-separated domain codes — e.g., 'Healthcare,Manufacturing'. Blank = all domains
Comma-separated persona codes — e.g., 'NewEmployee,Contractor'. Blank = all personas
JSON array mapping template placeholder tokens to Salesforce field API names
If true, triggers e-signature workflow post-generation
JSON array defining signer roles, order, and role type (Onboardee/Manager/Legal/HR)
Days after dispatch before unsigned document triggers escalation
Inactive templates excluded from DIE selection and checklist generation
HIPAA / OSHA / SOX / GDPR / FDA / ISO 9001 / ISO 45001 — for compliance tagging
Auto-incremented version number — previous versions retained for audit purposes
Primary language of template — used for locale-based template selection

Central index of all documents associated with an onboarding record. Each entry points to a Salesforce ContentDocument. Provides document lifecycle tracking and access control beyond native Salesforce Files.
Display name of the document — may differ from ContentDocument title
Salesforce ContentDocument ID — used to retrieve latest ContentVersion
Generated / Uploaded / External / Certificate / Identity Document / Compliance Record
Draft / Pending Signature / Signed / Verified / Expired / Archived
Links vault entry back to the checklist item that required this document
For certificates and licenses with defined validity periods
TODAY() > Expiry_Date__c — drives expiry alerts in B6 Compliance Engine
User (HR or Onboardee Experience Cloud user) who added this document
When the document was added to the vault
Not Required / Pending / Processing / Review Required / Accepted / Failed
Document version — incremented on each replace/update
Not Required / Pending / Partially Signed / Fully Signed / Expired / Delegated
Regulatory frameworks this document satisfies — populated from template on generation
One record per signing workflow per document. Tracks the overall multi-party signing lifecycle. Child records KT_Signer__c track individual signer status.
Configured / In Progress / Partially Signed / Fully Signed / Expired / Cancelled
Rollup of KT_Signer__c where Status = Signed
Deadline for all signatures — triggers escalation via Scheduled Flow
Sequential / Parallel — determines how signer invitations are dispatched
Provider-assigned ID (DocuSign envelope ID or Adobe agreement ID) — for webhook correlation
ContentDocument ID of the final signed document — populated on full completion
HR Admin who triggered the signing workflow
Child of KT_Signature_Request__c. One record per signer per signature request. Tracks individual signing status, delegation, and audit data.
Email address where signing invitation is sent
Onboardee / HR Admin / Hiring Manager / Legal Counsel / Compliance Officer
Numeric order — lower numbers sign first in Sequential mode
Pending / Invitation Sent / Viewed / Signed / Declined / Delegated / Expired
UTC timestamp when signature was captured
IPv4 or IPv6 address captured at signing moment — legal audit evidence
Browser/device user agent string at signing — legal audit evidence
Latitude/longitude if browser permission granted at signing
ContentDocument ID of signature image (canvas PNG)
Email of delegate if signer used delegation; populated on delegation action
Reason provided by original signer when delegating
Timestamp of last reminder — used to throttle reminder frequency
Created on every signature event. Immutable once created — provides the legal audit trail. Separate from KT_Signer__c to preserve integrity even if signer records are modified.
The signer record this audit event relates to
Invitation Sent / Document Viewed / Signature Captured / Signed / Delegated / Declined / Expired / Completed
UTC timestamp of the event — system-set, not editable
SHA-256 hash of (document content + signer email + timestamp) for tamper detection
One-time session token used for this signing session — invalidated post-signature
Tracks each OCR processing job submitted to the configured provider. Links the uploaded document to extracted data and HR review status.
AWS Textract / Azure Form Recognizer — populated from active CMT at job creation
Provider-assigned job/operation ID for async polling
Submitted / Processing / Succeeded / Failed / Review Required / Accepted / Rejected
Provider's classification of the document (e.g., 'Passport', 'Driver License')
Raw JSON response from OCR provider — stored for debugging and reprocessing
Structured key-value pairs post-mapping via KT_OCR_FieldMapping__mdt
Overall confidence score returned by provider (0–100%)
HR Admin notes when reviewing OCR results before acceptance
HR Admin who accepted or rejected the OCR extraction
Number of retry attempts — max 3 before creating manual review task
Tracks bulk document generation and dispatch jobs. Provides per-record visibility into batch processing status for HR Ops teams managing large onboarding cohorts.
Human-readable name for the bulk job — e.g., 'Sept 2026 Graduate Intake — Offer Letters'
Document template used for this bulk job
Total number of onboarding records in the batch
Onboarding records processed so far (success or failure)
Records that failed — details in KT_Bulk_Document_Error__c child records
Queued / Running / Partially Complete / Complete / Failed
Salesforce Batch Apex AsyncApexJob ID — for monitoring in Setup
HR Admin who triggered the bulk job
When all batches completed — null if still running
All configuration is stored as Custom Metadata — upgrade-safe, deployable via Metadata API, and manageable in Salesforce Setup by admins without code changes.
Maps domain + persona combinations to required document templates
Domain_Code__c, Persona_Code__c, Template_API_Name__c, Is_Required__c, SLA_Days__c, Display_Order__c
Maps OCR provider response fields to KT Onboarding Salesforce fields per document type
Document_Type__c, OCR_Provider__c, Provider_Field_Path__c, Target_Object__c, Target_Field__c, Confidence_Threshold__c
Active OCR provider configuration — switch between AWS Textract and Azure Form Recognizer without code changes
Provider_Name__c, Named_Credential__c, Is_Active__c, Endpoint_Path__c, Async_Polling_Interval__c, Max_Retries__c
Per-domain e-signature settings including default provider, expiry days, and legal validity requirements
Domain_Code__c, Default_Provider__c, Expiry_Days__c, Require_Geolocation__c, Require_IP__c, Legal_Notice_Text__c

This Apex class is the single entry point for all document generation. It accepts a KT_Onboarding__c ID, a KT_DocumentTemplate__c ID (or auto-resolves from CMT), merges Salesforce data, and produces a ContentVersion.

Primary generation method. Loads template binary from ContentDocument, executes merge field substitution, creates ContentVersion, creates KT_DocumentVault__c entry. Returns result wrapper with vault entry ID and ContentDocument ID.
Auto-generates all documents required by DIE for the given onboarding record. Called by B1 DIE on record creation. Iterates KT_Document_Request__c records with Direction = Outbound.
Replaces all {{FieldPath}} tokens in template with real Salesforce field values. Supports related object traversal up to 3 levels (e.g., {{KT_Onboarding__r.Contact__r.Account.Name}}).
@AuraEnabled method called by ktDocumentPreview LWC. Returns merge field values for real-time preview without full generation.

Document templates use double-brace tokens (e.g., {{Contact.FirstName}}, {{KT_Onboarding__c.Start_Date__c}}) that the generator resolves at runtime. The merge map is stored in KT_DocumentTemplate__c.Merge_Field_Map__c as JSON.
System-generated at runtime — supports offset arithmetic
Any field on KT_Onboarding__c or related objects — extensible
Real-time document preview component that shows HR admins a live preview of a generated document with merge fields substituted before committing to full generation.
ID of the KT_Onboarding__c record — passed from parent component or record page
Calls KT_DocumentGeneratorService.getPreviewData() to fetch resolved merge field values
Calls KT_DocumentGeneratorService.generateDocument() via Apex @AuraEnabled; navigates to vault entry on success
Fires on template picker selection change; re-fetches preview data for new template
Renders a styled HTML representation of the document with merge fields highlighted; shows unresolved tokens in red for HR visibility
For HR admins generating multiple documents or performing bulk operations, a guided Screen Flow provides a step-by-step experience replacing all OmniScript forms.
HR sees auto-suggested templates from DIE; can add/remove; sees required vs. optional classification
Stores template selection list in Flow variable
Shows ktDocumentPreview LWC for each selected template; HR reviews and confirms merge data accuracy
For templates requiring signature: HR enters signer emails, selects signing order (sequential/parallel), sets expiry date
Stores signature config in Flow variable
Summary screen with document count, signer count, estimated completion time; Generate button triggers Apex action
Invokes KT_DocumentGeneratorService and KT_ESignatureOrchestrator; shows progress spinner
Success: shows links to generated documents in vault. Error: shows per-document failure reasons with retry option

KT Sign is the native electronic signature capability built directly into the platform — no third-party e-signature service required for standard use cases. It renders within the Experience Cloud portal and internal Salesforce pages.
HTML5 Canvas-based signature pad rendered via ktSignaturePad LWC. Supports mouse, touch (iOS/Android), and stylus input. Renders at 2x pixel density on retina displays.

On signature submission: UTC timestamp captured server-side via Apex (not client-side to prevent manipulation); IP address captured via Apex HttpServletRequest equivalent (VF page wrapper for IP) or Experience Cloud session; device user agent from request headers; geolocation latitude/longitude from browser Geolocation API (user prompt; non-blocking if declined).
Canvas PNG exported as base64; decoded and stored as ContentVersion (VersionData Blob) encrypted at rest via Shield Platform Encryption. ContentDocument linked to KT_Signer__c.Signature_Image_Id__c.

KT_Signature_Audit__c record created immediately on signature capture — immutable (no update/delete sharing rules; audit object protected in managed package). SHA-256 hash of (document content hash + signer email + UTC timestamp) stored in Hash_Value__c for tamper detection.

One-time JWT session token issued when signer opens signing URL. Token consumed on first use; stored (hashed) in KT_Signer__c.Session_Token__c. Replaying the URL after signing returns an 'Already Signed' error page.

Configurable legal notice text displayed above signature pad — sourced from KT_Signature_Config__mdt.Legal_Notice_Text__c. Signer must check an 'I agree to sign electronically' checkbox (checkbox state also captured in audit record).

Creates KT_Signature_Request__c; creates KT_Signer__c records per signer; publishes Platform Event to trigger first invitation dispatch. Config includes signing order, signer list, expiry, provider selection.
Called by Platform Event handler and by completion callbacks. For Sequential mode: dispatches invitation only when previous signer has signed. For Parallel: dispatches all simultaneously.
@InvocableMethod called by Experience Cloud post-signature. Stores signature data, creates KT_Signature_Audit__c, updates KT_Signer__c status, calls dispatchNextInvitation or completeWorkflow.
handleDelegation(Id signerId, String delegateEmail, String reason)
Creates new KT_Signer__c for delegate; updates original signer status to Delegated; dispatches invitation to delegate; logs delegation event in KT_Signature_Audit__c.

Called when all signers have signed. Assembles final signed document (with all signature pages); creates completed ContentVersion; updates KT_DocumentVault__c status; fires Platform Event for downstream B6 compliance update.
Called by Scheduled Flow. Notifies HR of unsigned/expired requests; creates escalation task; optionally resends invitation depending on retry config in KT_Signature_Config__mdt.
For organizations with existing DocuSign agreements, the DocuSign connector replaces KT Sign for document dispatch while maintaining the same KT_Signature_Request__c and KT_Signer__c data model.
KT_DocuSignConnector.cls calls DocuSign eSign REST API /envelopes endpoint via Named Credential KT_DocuSign_NC. Document from ContentDocument base64-encoded in request body.
Signer tabs (signature, date, initials) placed via DocuSign tabs API based on KT_DocumentTemplate__c signer configuration.
DocuSign Connect webhook registered on envelope creation. Inbound REST endpoint KT_DocuSignWebhookHandler accepts status updates (sent/delivered/completed/declined).
On 'completed' webhook: completed document binary retrieved from DocuSign API, stored as new ContentVersion, KT_DocumentVault__c updated.
Regardless of provider (KT Sign or DocuSign), KT_Signer__c and KT_Signature_Audit__c records are always populated for consistent reporting.
Queries KT_Signature_Request__c where Status = In Progress AND Expiry_Date <= TODAY(). Calls KT_ESignatureOrchestrator.handleExpiryEscalation() for each.
Queries KT_Signer__c where Status = Invitation Sent AND Reminder_Sent_At < LAST_N_DAYS:2. Sends reminder email via Email Alert and increments Reminder_Sent_At.
Queries KT_Document_Request__c where Status = Not Started AND Due_Date <= TODAY+3 AND Reminder_Count < 3. Sends reminder to onboardee via portal notification + email.
On KT_Signer__c update where Status changed to Signed
Checks if all KT_Signer__c for parent KT_Signature_Request__c are Signed. If yes: updates KT_DocumentVault__c and KT_Document_Request__c to Complete.
The OCR pipeline converts uploaded identity documents, qualification certificates, and compliance documents into structured Salesforce data — eliminating manual data re-entry by HR teams.

B4 supports two OCR providers — AWS Textract and Azure Form Recognizer — switchable via KT_OCR_Provider_Config__mdt without code changes. The active provider is resolved at runtime by KT_OcrIngestionService.cls. Organizations can configure their preferred provider during installation or switch providers via the KT Setup Assistant LWC.
All OCR orchestration uses Apex + Platform Events + Record-Triggered Flow (replacing any OmniScript/Integration Procedure approach):
Onboardee or HR uploads document. ContentVersion created. Platform Event KT_Document_Uploaded__e published with ContentDocumentId, OnboardingId, DocumentType.

Flow subscribes to KT_Document_Uploaded__e. If DocumentType requires OCR (per KT_Document_Template_Config__mdt.Requires_OCR__c): creates KT_OCR_Job__c, calls KT_OcrIngestionService.submitJob() via Apex Action.
Queries KT_OCR_Provider_Config__mdt for active provider. Retrieves Named Credential. Determines provider-specific API endpoint and payload format.
POST to Textract AnalyzeDocument API (synchronous for documents < 10 pages) or StartDocumentAnalysis (async). Stores Job_Id__c for async polling.
POST to Azure Form Recognizer /analyze endpoint (always async). Returns Operation-Location URL stored in Job_Id__c for result polling.
Polls provider for job completion every 5 seconds (max 12 attempts = 60 seconds). On success: calls parseAndMap(). On max retries: marks OCR_Status__c = Failed, creates manual review task.

Maps raw JSON response fields to KT Onboarding fields using KT_OCR_FieldMapping__mdt records for the detected document type and active provider. Stores mapped fields in KT_OCR_Job__c.Extracted_Fields__c.

HR Admin sees extracted fields side-by-side with current record values. Field-by-field accept/reject. Confidence score colour-coded (green ≥90%, amber 70–89%, red <70%). One-click 'Accept All High Confidence' shortcut.
On HR acceptance: iterates accepted fields, updates KT_Onboarding__c (or related Contact) via dynamic DML. Immutably logs accepted fields in KT_OCR_Job__c.Reviewed_By__c and Reviewed_At__c.
After extraction: Copilot compares extracted values against existing record data. Flags discrepancies (e.g., extracted DOB differs from Contact.Birthdate). Posts alert to HR via in-app notification.
7.2 AWS Textract vs Azure Form Recognizer — Comparison
Synchronous (AnalyzeDocument) for <10 pages; Async (StartDocumentAnalysis) for larger
Always async: POST → poll Operation-Location URL
Tables, forms, key-value pairs, signatures, identity documents (Analyze Identity)
Pre-built models: ID document, invoice, receipt, W-2; custom model training available
Per-field confidence (0–1.0) returned in BlockType responses
Built-in ID Document model: Passport, Driver's License — 195+ countries
Custom adapter model training via Textract console
Custom neural model training via Azure portal or API
Per page analyzed — varies by feature (forms vs. tables vs. IDs)
Per page analyzed — varies by model type
Single synchronous POST or start-then-poll pattern
Preferred for organizations already in AWS ecosystem
Preferred for organizations in Microsoft/Azure ecosystem (common with Entra ID orgs)
Field mappings define how OCR provider response paths translate to Salesforce fields. HR Admins can add custom mappings via Salesforce Setup — no code change required.
OCR provider's detected document type label
JSON path or key in provider response (Textract key name or Azure field name)
Salesforce object to write the value to
API name of Salesforce field to populate
Minimum confidence % to auto-flag for acceptance (below = red in review UI)
Optional: UPPERCASE / TITLECASE / DATE_FORMAT / NONE — applied before writing

The document vault is a structured index layer on top of Salesforce Files (ContentDocument/ContentVersion). It provides enhanced metadata, access control, expiry tracking, and compliance tagging beyond native Salesforce Files capabilities.
All KT_DocumentVault__c records are Master-Detail children of KT_Onboarding__c. Vault is automatically scoped to the onboarding record — no cross-onboardee document visibility.

Each document re-upload creates a new ContentVersion linked to the same ContentDocument. Version_Number__c on KT_DocumentVault__c increments. Previous versions accessible via ContentVersion SOQL with IsLatest = false.

KT_Admin and KT_HR_User can view all vault entries. KT_Manager can view documents for their onboardees only (sharing rules based on Onboarding__r.Hiring_Manager__c). KT_Onboardee (Experience Cloud user) can view only their own documents, filtered by Document_Type__c visibility rules in CMT.

Is_Expired__c formula field drives a Scheduled Flow (KT_Document_Expiry_Alert) that creates tasks and sends email notifications 30/60/90 days before expiry. B6 Compliance Engine reads expiry status for compliance score calculation.
Shield Field Audit Trail configured on Status__c, Signature_Status__c, and OCR_Status__c fields with 10-year retention for regulated industries.
Uploaded documents scanned via configured security partner API (ClamAV-compatible endpoint) before ContentVersion is created. Files failing scan are quarantined (not deleted) and HR alerted.
Card-based document grid per onboardee. Documents grouped by Document_Type__c category with collapsible sections. Status badges (Pending / Signed / Verified / Expired) colour-coded.
PDF documents preview inline using PDF.js (loaded from Static Resource). DOCX files trigger download. Images display inline.
Side panel shows all ContentVersion records for a document. HR can restore a previous version or compare versions.

Drag-and-drop or file picker upload. File type validation (PDF/JPG/PNG/DOCX only; configurable via CMT). Max file size 25MB (Salesforce Files limit). Triggers OCR pipeline automatically if document type requires it.
Per-document actions: Preview, Download, Request Signature, Trigger OCR, Waive, Archive. Waive requires justification text input (minimum 20 characters).
Progress bar showing X of Y required documents complete. Missing required documents highlighted in red. Drives onboarding stage gate enforcement.
A simplified, mobile-first variant of the vault UI rendered in the Experience Cloud portal. Designed for onboardees — limited to their own documents, with guided upload flows.
Shows required documents as a checklist. Each item shows status (To Do / Uploaded / Signed / Approved). Tapping an item opens upload flow or signing link.
Step-by-step Screen Flow guides onboardees through document upload: select document type → upload file → confirm details → submit. Replaces OmniScript entirely.
Onboardees can see real-time status of each document (Pending HR Review, Verified, More Info Needed). HR can post a short note visible to the onboardee per document.
Full PWA offline support for checklist viewing. Upload requires connectivity. Touch-optimized UI with large tap targets and thumb-zone navigation.
The bulk processing engine enables HR teams to dispatch documents to large onboarding cohorts (e.g., graduate intake classes, seasonal retail hiring waves) without manual intervention.

Apex Batch class implementing Database.Batchable<SObject> and Database.AllowsCallouts. Batch size 25 records (conservative to stay within callout governor limits). Each execute() generates documents and dispatches e-signature invitations for a chunk of onboarding records.

Queueable Apex class chained from the batch for post-processing tasks: updating KT_Bulk_Document_Job__c counters, publishing Platform Events for Data Cloud streaming, and triggering Agentforce HR Copilot notification on job completion.

Service class providing @AuraEnabled methods for the bulk send Screen Flow: validateCohort() checks all target records for merge field completeness before job starts; getBulkJobStatus() provides real-time progress for the monitoring LWC.

Real-time job monitoring dashboard. Shows progress bar (processed/total), success/failure counts, estimated completion time, and per-record error details. Auto-refreshes every 10 seconds using Lightning Refresh API.
9.2 Bulk Send Screen Flow — HR Wizard
HR selects target onboarding records via list view filter, date range, or cohort tag (KT_Onboarding__c.Cohort__c field). Count displayed. Maximum 10,000 records per job.
HR selects document template from active library. Preview shown with sample merge data from first record in cohort.
Calls KT_BulkDocumentJobService.validateCohort() — identifies records with missing merge data. HR can exclude flagged records or proceed with available data.
If template requires signature: HR configures signing settings applied uniformly to all records in cohort (provider, expiry, signing order).
KT_Bulk_Document_Job__c created; KT_BulkDocumentBatch.execute() scheduled via Database.executeBatch(). HR redirected to ktBulkDocumentMonitor LWC.
Document generation with external e-sign API calls per batch chunk
Batch size 25 records max per execute(); each record = max 2 callouts (generate + dispatch). Well within 100 limit.
Large DOCX template binary processing and merge field resolution
Template binary loaded once per execute() from Static Resource or ContentDocument cache. Merge resolution uses indexed SOQL with LIMIT.
Large document binaries in memory during generation
Documents processed and written to ContentVersion one at a time within execute(). Batch size kept at 25 to prevent heap accumulation.
Each batch chunk chains a single Queueable for post-processing. Batch completes all generation work; Queueable only handles lightweight status updates.
Creating KT_DocumentVault__c, KT_Document_Request__c updates, ContentVersion inserts
Batch size 25 × max 4 DML rows per record = 100 DML rows per execute(). Well within limits.
KT_DocuSignConnector.cls — envelope creation, status, document retrieval
AWS Textract API (us-east-1 or configured region)
AWS Signature V4 (Access Key / Secret in Named Credential)
Azure Cognitive Services Form Recognizer endpoint
Configurable virus scan endpoint (ClamAV REST or commercial)
KT_VirusScanService.cls — called on document upload before ContentVersion creation
Data Cloud streaming ingestion; B6 Compliance Engine flow
B6 Compliance Engine flow; Data Cloud; B10 Analytics dataset refresh
Record-Triggered Flow → Update KT_DocumentVault__c.OCR_Status__c; Agentforce HR Copilot action
Receives envelope status updates (sent/delivered/signed/completed/declined). Correlates via External_Envelope_Id__c. Updates KT_Signer__c and KT_DocumentVault__c.
Receives agreement status events. Same processing pattern as DocuSign handler.
Receives async OCR completion notification. Triggers result retrieval and field mapping.

Compares extracted field values in KT_OCR_Job__c.Extracted_Fields__c against existing Contact and KT_Onboarding__c field values. Flags discrepancies with explanation. Posts structured alert to HR via in-app notification: 'OCR extracted DOB 15-Jun-1985 but Contact record shows 16-Jun-1985 — please review.'
HR Admin asks: 'What documents are missing for [Name]?'
Queries KT_Document_Request__c for onboarding record. Lists incomplete required documents, overdue reminders, and waived documents with reasons. Suggests next actions.
HR Admin asks: 'How is the September intake document send going?'
Queries KT_Bulk_Document_Job__c for recent jobs. Returns completion percentage, failure count with failure reason summary, and estimated finish time.
KT_Signature_Request__c approaching expiry (Scheduled Flow triggers agent action)
Generates escalation notification for HR: lists unsigned documents by signer name and days to expiry. Suggests delegation or manual follow-up.

Agent cross-references KT_DocumentVault__c completeness against KT_Compliance_Rule__mdt requirements for the onboardee's domain, persona, and location. Reports which regulatory framework documents are outstanding.
Scans KT_DocumentVault__c where Expiry_Date__c is within 30/60/90 days. Generates per-onboardee expiry report for compliance officer review. Integrated with B6 compliance dashboard.

On demand: generates a structured compliance document vault report per onboardee — lists all documents, status, signature dates, OCR verification status — formatted as a downloadable PDF via B4 Document Generator.
PII in OCR data passed to LLM

Einstein Trust Layer enforces PII masking before OCR-extracted data is included in Agentforce prompt context. Passport numbers, government IDs, and dates of birth are masked with [REDACTED] in prompt grounding.
Document binary content is never passed to Agentforce. Only structured field values from KT_OCR_Job__c.Extracted_Fields__c (post-mapping, pre-write) are used for discrepancy comparison prompts.
All Agentforce interactions logged in Einstein Trust Layer audit log. KT_Signature_Audit__c and KT_OCR_Job__c records note when Agentforce review was triggered and by which agent.

Applied to: KT_Signer__c.IP_Address__c, KT_Signer__c.Geolocation, KT_OCR_Job__c.Extracted_JSON__c, KT_DocumentVault__c fields containing PII references. Customer-managed encryption keys (BYOK) available in Ultra tier.
10-year field history on: KT_DocumentVault__c.Status__c, KT_Signature_Request__c.Status__c, KT_OCR_Job__c.Status__c, KT_Signer__c.Status__c. Mandatory for SOX and HIPAA regulated customers.
All vault access, document downloads, OCR job submissions, and signature captures logged via Salesforce Event Monitoring. Exportable to SIEM tools (Splunk, Sumo Logic) via Event Log File API.
ContentVersion.VersionData stored encrypted at rest via Salesforce Shield. Signature image ContentVersions encrypted with the same shield key as onboardee PII.

All uploaded documents passed through KT_VirusScanService.cls before ContentVersion creation. Files failing scan quarantined in KT_DocumentVault__c with Status__c = Quarantined; HR alerted immediately.
Full CRUD on all B4 objects. Manage document templates. Configure OCR mappings. Trigger bulk jobs. Access all vault entries.
Create/Read/Edit KT_Document_Request__c, KT_DocumentVault__c, KT_Signature_Request__c, KT_Signer__c. Read KT_Signature_Audit__c. No delete on audit objects.
Read-only on all B4 objects. Generate audit-ready reports. Access compliance document vault views. Cannot modify document or signature records.
Read KT_Document_Request__c and KT_DocumentVault__c for own team onboardees only (sharing rules). No access to OCR job records or bulk processing.

Read own KT_Document_Request__c (status only). Upload documents (creates ContentVersion + KT_DocumentVault__c via Apex guest context). Read own vault entries filtered by visibility rules. No access to any other onboardee's data.
All SOQL queries in B4 Apex classes use WITH SHARING and bind variable parameters — no SOQL injection vectors
Named Credentials used for all external callouts — no credentials in Apex, no credentials in Custom Metadata
All LWC components use Salesforce framework auto-escaping — no innerHTML assignment in JavaScript
Apex REST handlers (webhook receivers) validate inbound payload signatures (HMAC-SHA256) before processing
OCR provider API keys stored exclusively in Named Credentials — not in Custom Metadata or Static Resources
Virus scan executed before ContentVersion DML insert — no unscanned files enter the org
KT_Signature_Audit__c protected by managed package sharing rules — no delete access for any permission set including KT_Admin
Any external agent can trigger document ingestion for a given onboarding record. Input: onboardingId, documentType, ContentDocumentId. Output: vaultEntryId, ocrJobId (if triggered).
Returns structured vault contents for an onboarding record. Output: array of {documentName, documentType, status, signatureStatus, expiryDate}.
Triggers e-signature workflow for a given document vault entry. Input: vaultId, signerList, signingOrder, expiryDays. Output: signatureRequestId.
AXL signing tiles: KT_Signature_Request__c signing pending state rendered as interactive Slack/Teams card — signer taps card to open mobile signing flow
DocuSign AgentExchange MCP connector replaces custom DocuSign Named Credential integration — leverages first-class DocuSign MCP tool from AgentExchange marketplace

Trusted Agent Identity: high-value signatures (SOX conflict of interest disclosures, HIPAA authorizations) require mobile-push biometric confirmation before KT_ESignatureOrchestrator.completeWorkflow() proceeds
All B4 development tasks mapped to sprints in alignment with the KT Onboarding Enterprise Sprint Plan (Sprints 1–9).
Create KT_Document_Request__c object with all fields defined in Section 4.1
Create KT_DocumentTemplate__c object with all fields including Merge_Field_Map__c JSON
Create KT_DocumentVault__c object — master-detail to KT_Onboarding__c
Create KT_Document_Template_Config__mdt — domain/persona to template mapping
Create KT_OCR_FieldMapping__mdt — provider field to Salesforce field mapping
Configure Named Credentials: KT_DocuSign_NC, KT_AWS_Textract_NC, KT_Azure_FormRecognizer_NC, KT_VirusScan_NC
Build KT_DocumentChecklistService.cls — called by DIE to create KT_Document_Request__c records on onboarding creation
Build ktDocumentPreview LWC — real-time preview with merge field substitution and unresolved field highlighting
Build Screen Flow: Document Generation Wizard (5 screens as per Section 5.4)
Build ktSignaturePad LWC — HTML5 canvas signature, touch/stylus support, legal notice, session token validation
Build KT_ESignatureOrchestrator.cls — all methods per Section 6.2
Build ktESignatureConfig LWC — signing order configuration, signer role assignment, expiry input
Build KT_DocuSignConnector.cls — envelope creation, tab placement, webhook registration
Build KT_DocuSignWebhookHandler.cls — Apex REST @RestResource for DocuSign Connect callbacks
Build KT_ESignatureService.cls — IP capture wrapper, geolocation collection, signature hash generation
Build ktDocumentVault LWC — HR vault grid, version history, quick actions, checklist completion bar
Build ktDocumentVaultPortal LWC — onboardee portal variant, mobile-first, guided upload Screen Flow
Build KT_BulkDocumentBatch.cls + KT_BulkDocumentQueueable.cls — governor limit safe bulk generation
Build ktBulkDocumentMonitor LWC — real-time job progress dashboard with auto-refresh
Build Screen Flow: Bulk Send Wizard (5 screens per Section 9.2)
Build Scheduled Flow: KT_ESignature_Expiry_Check (daily, triggers handleExpiryEscalation)
Build Scheduled Flow: KT_ESignature_Reminder (daily, reminder emails to pending signers)
Build Scheduled Flow: KT_DocumentRequest_Reminder (daily, portal + email reminders for missing docs)
Build Record-Triggered Flow: KT_DocumentVault_StatusSync (on KT_Signer__c update, cascade to vault/request)
Publish Platform Events: KT_Document_Uploaded__e, KT_Document_Generated__e, KT_Signature_Complete__e
Build KT_OcrIngestionService.cls — submitJob(), submitToTextract(), submitToAzure(), parseAndMap(), applyExtraction()
Build KT_OcrPollingBatch.cls (Queueable) — async polling with max retry and failure handling
Build KT_OcrCallbackHandler.cls — Apex REST @RestResource for async OCR completion notifications
Build ktOCRReview LWC — field-by-field accept/reject, confidence score colour coding, Accept All High Confidence action
Publish KT_OCR_Job_Complete__e Platform Event and wire to HR Copilot Agentforce action
Configure Agentforce KT HR Copilot B4 actions: OCR discrepancy detection, gap analysis, bulk job status, signing escalation
Configure Agentforce KT Compliance Auditor B4 actions: missing compliance docs, expiring documents, audit report generation
Build KT_VirusScanService.cls — pre-upload virus scan via Named Credential; quarantine logic
Configure Shield Platform Encryption on PII fields (KT_Signer__c, KT_OCR_Job__c)
Configure Field Audit Trail on all audit-critical status fields (10-year retention)
Build Scheduled Flow: KT_Document_Expiry_Alert (30/60/90-day expiry notifications)
Populate 200+ KT_DocumentTemplate__c records across 8 domains — template library seeding
Populate KT_OCR_FieldMapping__mdt records for Passport, Driver's License, Professional License (both providers)
Expose kt_upload_document, kt_get_document_vault, kt_dispatch_signing MCP tools (Headless 360)
Upgrade DocuSign connector to AgentExchange MCP tool (Sprint 8 add-on)
Trusted Agent Identity for SOX/HIPAA high-value signatures
AXL signing tiles — Slack/Teams interactive signing card with mobile signing flow
React-based document preview player via Vibes 2.0 (Sprint 9 Beta)

Feature gating is enforced via KT_Feature_Gate__mdt records checked in KT_DocumentGeneratorService.cls, KT_ESignatureOrchestrator.cls, and KT_OcrIngestionService.cls before executing any gated operation. If the subscriber org's tier does not include the requested feature, a KT_FeatureGateException is raised with an in-app upgrade prompt linking to the AppExchange checkout flow.
< 3 seconds from button click to ContentVersion created
Apex CPU time logs + LWC LCP measurement
< 8 seconds from upload to HR review screen available
Platform Event timestamp delta: KT_Document_Uploaded__e → KT_OCR_Job_Complete__e
< 5 seconds from HR confirmation to email delivered to signer
SMTP delivery timestamp vs. KT_Signer__c dispatch timestamp
< 2 seconds for up to 50 vault entries
Lighthouse Performance audit on Lightning record page
< 3 seconds from file drop to ContentVersion created
Support 100+ concurrent signing sessions without degradation
Load test via Salesforce Performance Testing guidelines
KT_Signature_Audit__c: minimum 7 years (10-year Field Audit Trail for HIPAA/SOX orgs)
Shield Field Audit Trail configuration audit
Contract Lifecycle Management — end-to-end management of document creation, negotiation, execution, and storage.
Custom Metadata Type — Salesforce metadata record used to store configuration data that is upgrade-safe and deployable via Metadata API.
Salesforce standard objects representing files. ContentDocument is the parent; ContentVersion is each version of the file.
KT Onboarding's native e-signature capability — canvas-based, legally valid, no third-party dependency.
Salesforce secure credential store for external API authentication — used for all B4 external callouts.
Optical Character Recognition — AI-powered extraction of structured text data from scanned document images.
Salesforce event-driven messaging mechanism — used in B4 for asynchronous document lifecycle event propagation.
Salesforce declarative Flow type that renders user-facing guided experiences — replaces OmniScript in B4.
Salesforce add-on that encrypts data at rest using customer-managed keys — applied to PII fields in B4.
Salesforce Sprint 8 feature — adds mobile-push biometric authorization for high-value agent actions.
Agent Experience Layer — Salesforce technology that renders Agentforce agent interactions natively in Slack and Microsoft Teams.
KT Onboarding Enterprise Design Document v2.0 — parent architecture document covering all 13 features
KT Onboarding Enterprise Feature Summary List v2.0 — B1–B13 feature overview
KT Onboarding Enterprise Product Blueprint v3.0 — Sprint Plan and feature deep-dives
KT Onboarding API Reference (Swagger/OpenAPI 3.0) — B4 endpoint documentation
KT Onboarding Data Dictionary — complete field-level documentation for all 28 custom objects including B4 objects
KT Onboarding AppExchange Security Review Submission — security attestation covering B4 OCR and e-signature components

Initial B4 standalone design document — full end-to-end coverage of Document Management & CLM with no OmniStudio dependency. AWS Textract + Azure Form Recognizer dual OCR provider design. 49 development tasks across 9 sprints.
KT Onboarding Enterprise | B4 Document Management & CLM | Confidential — Internal Use Only | April 2026
